title: pedilonum quadriquetrum
intro: Pedilonum quadriquetrum, also known as the "four-angled pedilonum" or "Australian finger root," is a perennial plant native to Australia, characterized by its finger-shaped roots and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Pedilonum quadriquetrum include reducing inflammation and pain, treating wounds and infections, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tincture, infusion, or poultice for topical application, or as a decoction for internal use.
